verses.text: Moses and Aaron ask Pharaoh, 'Let my people go'; he scoffs, 'Who is the LORD?' Defiance. The proud king knows not God.
Moses and Aaron ask Pharaoh, 'Let my people go'; he scoffs, 'Who is the LORD?' Defiance. The proud king knows not God.
Moses and Aaron ask Pharaoh, 'Let my people go'; he scoffs, 'Who is the LORD?' Defiance. The proud king knows not God.
Pharaoh accuses them of making the people idle from their work. Hostile. He counts worship mere laziness.
Pharaoh accuses them of making the people idle from their work. Hostile. He counts worship mere laziness.
He commands no more straw be given, yet the same tale of bricks. Cruel burden. Tyranny answers God's call with heavier loads.
He commands no more straw be given, yet the same tale of bricks. Cruel burden. Tyranny answers God's call with heavier loads.
He commands no more straw be given, yet the same tale of bricks. Cruel burden. Tyranny answers God's call with heavier loads.
He commands no more straw be given, yet the same tale of bricks. Cruel burden. Tyranny answers God's call with heavier loads.
The people scatter to gather stubble; the officers are beaten when the tale falls short. Beaten. The innocent suffer for an impossible demand.

The people scatter to gather stubble; the officers are beaten when the tale falls short. Beaten. The innocent suffer for an impossible demand.
The people scatter to gather stubble; the officers are beaten when the tale falls short. Beaten. The innocent suffer for an impossible demand.
The officers cry to Pharaoh, but he calls them idle and refuses relief. No mercy. Their appeal only hardens him.

The officers cry to Pharaoh, but he calls them idle and refuses relief. No mercy. Their appeal only hardens him.
They meet Moses and Aaron and blame them for making them 'abhorred' before Pharaoh. Misplaced blame. Trials make even the freed turn on their deliverers.
They meet Moses and Aaron and blame them for making them 'abhorred' before Pharaoh. Misplaced blame. Trials make even the freed turn on their deliverers.
Moses returns to God: 'why is it that thou hast sent me?... neither hast thou delivered thy people.' Honest complaint. He brings his confusion straight to God.
